question: Will Atletico Madrid finish in the top 4 of the La Liga 2025–26 standings?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the La Liga standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the La Liga standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official La Liga tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 La Liga season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from La Liga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona's explosive 10 wins in 12 La Liga matches have vaulted them to the top with 30 points, fueling trader consensus on their top-four lock for Champions League qualification, especially after a dominant 4-0 Clasico rout of Real Madrid. Madrid sits second at 27 points but grapples with key absences like Carvajal and Militao sidelined long-term, exposing defensive vulnerabilities amid recent draws. Atletico Madrid (26 points) and Athletic Bilbao (23 points) lurk closely, bolstered by Griezmann's form and Bilbao's home strength, though both face grueling schedules including Madrid derbies and rival clashes before the World Cup break. Momentum favors Barca, but upsets remain likely in this compressed race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayer Leverkusen's unbeaten run and Bundesliga title clinch have locked in their top-four finish, with Bayern Munich safely ensconced in second on superior goal difference and form. The intense race for third and fourth hinges on VfB Stuttgart (63 points), Borussia Dortmund (58), and RB Leipzig (56), separated by just seven points with four matchdays left. Upcoming fixtures pit Dortmund against Leipzig directly, while Stuttgart faces Bayern away—key tests shaping implied probabilities in this tight UCL qualification battle.

question: Will Leverkusen finish in the top 4 of the Bundesliga 2025–26 standings?
description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the Bundesliga standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the Bundesliga standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official Bundesliga tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: PSG's commanding lead with 36 points after 15 matches has all but secured their top-4 spot and Champions League qualification, shifting trader consensus toward the fierce battle for the remaining three berths where Monaco (28 pts), Marseille (27), Nice (25), and Lille (24) cluster tightly. Recent developments include Monaco's resilient away form despite a midweek draw, Marseille's morale-boosting derby win over Lyon, and Lille's three-match winning streak fueled by Jonathan David's scoring surge. Upcoming head-to-heads like Monaco-Lille on December 8 and Marseille-Nice on December 15 could swing implied probabilities, while schedule density and winter injuries loom as key risks in this unpredictable Ligue 1 grind.

question: Taylor Swift pregnant before marriage?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Taylor Swift announces that she is pregnant before she announces that she is married to Travis Kelce. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements will qualify. Pregnancy or marriage announcements that are not credible, for example jokes, will not suffice.

If Taylor Swift does not announce that she is pregnant or married to Travis Kelce by August 31, 2026 ET, or their engagement is otherwise broken off, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be statements from Taylor Swift or her representatives; however, a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be used.

question: Will Bernie endorse James Talarico for TX-Sen by Nov 2 2026 ET?
description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate Bernie Sanders announces that he will vote for or endorses for the listed election.

If Bernie Sanders does not announce an endorsement by the day before the scheduled election at 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Bernie Sanders or one of his representatives, or a consensus of credible reporting of Bernie Sanders' endorsement.

events.title: Who will Bernie endorse?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Bernie Sanders' endorsement carries weight among progressive Democrats, with trader consensus on Polymarket reflecting his likely alignment with the party's nominee amid recent shifts. On July 28, 2024, Sanders officially endorsed Kamala Harris for president, following President Biden's withdrawal and endorsement of her on July 21, solidifying her position as the de facto Democratic standard-bearer. Prior sentiment favored Harris due to Sanders' past support for her prosecutorial record evolving into policy overlap on issues like Medicare for All and climate action. Key factors include Sanders' history of late-cycle endorsements in 2016 and 2020, his recent criticisms of Biden's Gaza policy, and the Democratic National Convention August 19-22, where formal nomination could prompt further progressive backing. Markets now price in minimal uncertainty post-endorsement.

If Bernie Sanders does not announce an endorsement by the day before the scheduled election at 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Bernie Sanders or one of his representatives, or a consensus of credible reporting of Bernie Sanders' endorsement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bernie Sanders' endorsement carries weight among progressive Democrats, with trader consensus on Polymarket reflecting his likely alignment with the party's nominee amid recent shifts. On July 28, 2024, Sanders officially endorsed Kamala Harris for president, following President Biden's withdrawal and endorsement of her on July 21, solidifying her position as the de facto Democratic standard-bearer. Prior sentiment favored Harris due to Sanders' past support for her prosecutorial record evolving into policy overlap on issues like Medicare for All and climate action. Key factors include Sanders' history of late-cycle endorsements in 2016 and 2020, his recent criticisms of Biden's Gaza policy, and the Democratic National Convention August 19-22, where formal nomination could prompt further progressive backing. Markets now price in minimal uncertainty post-endorsement.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's potential endorsement in the Senate Republican leader race remains the dominant driver of trader sentiment, with implied probabilities heavily favoring Sen. Rick Scott at around 60% amid his recent meeting with the president-elect and Trump's past criticisms of rival John Thune. Following the GOP's Senate majority win, the contest pits establishment figures Thune and John Cornyn against Scott's MAGA-aligned challenge, amplified by conservative pressure groups like the Club for Growth urging Trump to back an outsider. Recent Truth Social posts from Trump praising Scott's energy have boosted his odds, while uncertainty lingers as Trump has not formally committed. Traders eye the closed-door GOP conference vote on November 13, which could shift dramatically based on Trump's final signal.

question: Macron out by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Emmanuel Macron ceases to be President of France for any length of time between September 14, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET (inclusive). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Macron departs from office before the expiry date, the market should resolve immediately.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the government of France, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Macron out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Emmanuel Macron ceases to be President of France for any length of time between January 2 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET (inclusive). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Macron departs from office before the expiry date, the market should resolve immediately.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from the government of France, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent governmental instability in France anchors trader consensus on President Emmanuel Macron's tenure timeline, highlighted by the National Assembly's no-confidence vote ousting Prime Minister Michel Barnier on December 4, 2024—the first since 1962. Centrist ally François Bayrou's appointment as replacement PM on December 13 provides temporary stabilization for Macron's fragmented coalition, but his minority government confronts an imminent investiture vote and fraught 2025 budget negotiations amid left-right deadlock. Macron, restricted from new parliamentary dissolution until June 2025, affirms commitment through 2027, yet fiscal strains and opposition maneuvers sustain uncertainty over earlier ouster risks.

question: Starmer out by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between September 14, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

events.title: Starmer out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Keir Starmer ceases to be the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom for any period of time between February 2 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Keir Starmer's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be the government of the UK, however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Keir Starmer tenure markets reflects heightened skepticism fueled by Labour's post-budget poll collapse, with support dipping below 30% in recent MRPs amid public fury over tax hikes on employers and pensioner winter fuel cuts. Chancellor Rachel Reeves' October 30 fiscal statement triggered farmer protests, MP rebellions on welfare bills, and Reform UK's surge to 20%+. Starmer quelled internal threats by reshuffling cabinet and winning conference leadership ballot, though low turnout underscored fragility. Upcoming local elections in May 2025 and potential by-elections loom as catalysts; sustained discontent could prompt no-confidence moves or leadership bids.

question: Will the next UK election be called by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the date of the next UK general election is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For this market to resolve to "Yes" it is only necessary that the election date be declared, not that the election actually occur within the market timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: UK election called by...?
events.description: This is a market on predicting the date when the UK general election will be officially called.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rishi Sunak's surprise announcement on May 22 calling a snap UK general election for July 4 has decisively shaped trader consensus on Polymarket, with odds heavily favoring resolution under his initiative amid Conservative Party infighting and dismal polls. Labour under Keir Starmer leads national surveys by over 20 points, boosting bets on a landslide shift, while Reform UK's Nigel Farage siphons right-wing votes. Recent catalysts include manifesto releases highlighting tax and immigration divides, plus a televised debate where Sunak faltered. Traders eye final hustings and postal vote tallies before polls close July 4, as turnout and regional swings could narrow the implied 95%+ probability of no Conservative majority.

question: Will Mallory McMorrow win the 2026 Michigan Democratic Primary?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Michigan Democratic Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket favors Mallory McMorrow at 63% implied probability to win Michigan's Democratic Senate primary, driven by recent internal polls showing her leading among likely voters and strong grassroots fundraising surpassing $2 million. Abdul El-Sayed holds 19.5% on momentum from progressive endorsements and his prior statewide name recognition as former health director, while Haley Stevens trails at 17% bolstered by congressional experience and union backing. Recent developments include McMorrow's viral social media push amplifying moderate appeal and a key EMILY's List nod last week, shifting odds upward; Stevens' camp countered with labor rallies. With the August 6 primary nearing, early voting data and final debates could sway the crowded field.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Graham Platner (75%) as the Maine Democratic U.S. Senate primary winner on June 11, driven by his status as the only candidate who has officially filed paperwork with the state, solidifying his frontrunner position amid a compressed timeline. Governor Janet Mills holds 23.5% implied probability amid ongoing speculation of a potential late entry, though she has not declared and faces term-limit considerations after her gubernatorial re-election bid. events.title: South Carolina Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from South Carolina.

If no 2026 South Carolina Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Lindsey Graham's 84% implied probability as South Carolina Republican Senate primary winner stems from his incumbency advantage, strong fundraising exceeding $10 million, and history of surviving intraparty challenges, including a 2016 runoff victory. Recent entrant Paul Dans, ex-Project 2025 director, has surged to 10.5% on buzz from conservative activists criticizing Graham's bipartisan votes, while businessman Mark Lynch holds 5.1% amid modest grassroots support and Thomas Murphy trails at 0.3%. No polls yet exist for the June 2026 contest, but trader consensus reflects Graham's name recognition edge, with Dans' August 2024 launch as the key catalyst lifting challengers slightly ahead of candidate filing deadlines.

question: Will AppLovin acquire TikTok?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is officially announced that TikTok will be, has been, or is being acquired by or merged with the listed entity, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by TikTok will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

If TikTok is part of an acquisition or merger involving the specified entity, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether it is the sole buyer or partner.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from TikTok or the listed entity, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be use

events.title: Who will acquire TikTok?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes"  if it is officially announced that that the listed individual, either personally or through an entity, enters into an agreement with TikTok to acquire its US operations by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the acquisition is ultimately completed.

The entity entering into the agreement does not need to be controlled by the listed individual; their involvement as a partial owner, investor, or similar role will qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the listed individual and/or TikTok, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on TikTok's potential acquisition hinges on President-elect Trump's pledge to extend the January 19, 2025, divestiture deadline and broker a deal preserving the app's U.S. operations, slashing implied ban probabilities to under 20%. Recent meetings between Trump and TikTok's CEO, alongside bids from Oracle, Perplexity AI, Frank McCourt's consortium, and Shark Tank's Kevin O'Leary, fuel optimism for a U.S.-led buyer amid competitive dynamics where tech giants hesitate over Beijing's approval veto power. Key watchpoints include Trump's January 20 inauguration for executive action and CFIUS review outcomes, though historical China-U.S. tensions underscore resolution risks for traders holding positions.

question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

events.title: Brazil Presidential Election
events.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a razor-thin lead for President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 41.5% over Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's 38.9% in the 2026 Brazil presidential race, driven by persistent economic headwinds like high inflation and fiscal deficits eroding Lula's approval amid polarized voter bases. Recent polls from Datafolha and Ipec show the duo neck-and-neck, with right-wing consolidation around Flávio—Jair Bolsonaro's son—bolstering conservative turnout despite the ex-president's ineligibility until 2030. Lower odds for Ratinho Júnior (6.3%) and others highlight fragmented fields. Separation could emerge from Q3 GDP data, Supreme Court rulings on electoral eligibility, or major endorsements before 2026 primaries, as traders weigh Lula's incumbency against anti-PT sentiment.

question: Will Kylian Mbappe be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?
description:
This market will resolve according to the top goalscorer of the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: UEFA Champions League: Top Scorer
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the top goalscorer of the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé commands 72% implied probability as UEFA Champions League top scorer due to his four goals in three league-phase matches for juggernaut Real Madrid, including key strikes against Dortmund, fueling trader confidence in his finishing prowess amid the team's title-favorite status. Harry Kane trails at 22.5% with five goals, highlighted by a brace in Bayern Munich's dominant 4-0 win over Celtic, but faces stiffer competition from Dortmund's Serhou Guirassy (seven goals, unpriced here). Recent form edges Mbappé ahead via Real's high-scoring attack and favorable run-ins, while lower options like Alvarez (Man City) and Yamal (Barcelona) lag on volume despite team momentum; odds reflect crowd wisdom on sustained output over early volatility.

question: Will any presidential candidate win outright in the first round of the Brazil election?
description:
Presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Brazil on October 4, 2026. A second round will be held if no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any candidate wins this election in the first round. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the result of the first round of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest Brazilian presidential polls for the October 2026 election show no candidate approaching the 50% plus one vote threshold required for a first-round outright victory, with leading contenders like São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas polling around 30-35%, President Lula da Silva in the mid-20s, and others like Michelle Bolsonaro or Sergio Moro trailing further in a fragmented field. This distribution mirrors historical patterns where runoffs have occurred in most cycles since 1994, except rare outright wins like Dilma Rousseff's in 2014. Recent Datafolha and Quaest surveys reinforce trader consensus at 88% "No," amid economic headwinds and coalition shifts, with no major consolidation signals ahead of party conventions in mid-2026.

description:
General elections are scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026. If no candidate wins outright in the first round, the top two candidates proceed to a runoff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed candidate advances to Brazil's presidential runoff election or otherwise wins outright in the first round. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the president is decided in the first round of the election or the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

events.title: Which candidates will advance to Brazil's presidential runoff?
events.description:

If the president is decided in the first round of the election or the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: President Lula da Silva leads national polls for Brazil's 2026 presidential election first round, with trader consensus on Polymarket implying high probabilities for him and São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas to advance to the October 25 runoff, based on recent Datafolha and Quaest surveys showing Lula at 37-42% and Tarcísio at 18-22%. Lula's incumbency edge and Northeast dominance offset economic headwinds like inflation, while Tarcísio gains from Southeast momentum and Bolsonaro allies' support despite the ex-president's ongoing ineligibility through 2030. Recent TSE rulings barring other right-wing figures narrowed the field, boosting these frontrunners. Key watches: Lula's re-election announcement by April 2026, party conventions, and Q1 economic indicators that could shift regional voter turnout dynamics.

question: Will China blockade Taiwan by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China (People's Republic of China) announces it has established or otherwise de facto establishes an arial or naval blockade for the territory of Taiwan (Republic of China) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying blockade is:
- Prevents the normal ingress or egress of foreign commercial traffic to or from Taiwan Island’s main ports or airports by threat or use of force for ≥ 24 hours.
- Covers part or whole of the main island of Taiwan (Formosa).
- Is declared and enforced, de facto (e.g., it is established that China is blocking a significant portion of foreign commercial traffic, as described above, by a wide consensus of credible reporting regardless of whether China has issued a statement or not), or China-issued navigation/airspace prohibitions covering Taiwan's main island's approach lanes that are actively enforced so that most foreign commercial access is denied.

A qualifying blockade is not:
- Military or naval exercises or drills (established with warning areas or NOTAMs that do not actively stop third-country ships/aircraft and do not materially deny access).
- Purely economic or coercive measures (e.g., sanctions, customs delays, fishing bans, cyber/GPS jamming) without physical interdiction or enforced closure).
- Weather/accident-related closures or voluntary rerouting by operators absent PRC enforcement.
- Islet-only incidents that do not involve the main island of Taiwan.
- Seizure or inspection of a single vessel/aircraft by itself, unless part of an enforced pattern that denies access as defined above.

The resolution source for this market will be a broad consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders price a China blockade of Taiwan by June 30 at just 7.5% likelihood, driven by the absence of any military mobilization or official signals from Beijing indicating imminent action. Recent PLA exercises around the Taiwan Strait, launched after President Lai Ching-te's May inauguration, concluded without escalation to blockades or patrols restricting navigation, signaling posturing rather than preparation. U.S. intelligence assessments highlight China's military gaps, projecting no invasion or blockade capability before 2027 amid high economic costs and deterrence from Washington. Cross-strait tensions persist via routine patrols, but no catalysts like troop buildups or deadlines justify near-term risks, aligning with stable market consensus.

question: Will Trump visit China by March 31?
description:
If U.S. President Donald Trump visits China by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Trump physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the listed country. Whether or not Trump enters the country's airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from government of the United States of America, official information from Trump or released by his verified social media accounts (e.g. https://twitter.com/POTUS), however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for President-elect Trump visiting China soon, driven primarily by the absence of any official announcements from his transition team or Beijing amid escalating tariff threats and hawkish rhetoric on trade imbalances. Recent US-China tensions persist following their November 2024 APEC summit in Peru, where leaders reaffirmed dialogue but announced no travel plans. Historical precedent shows Trump's 2017 Beijing trip occurred months after inauguration, yet current focus on cabinet selections and domestic priorities reduces near-term likelihood. Key upcoming catalysts include Trump's January 20, 2025, inauguration and early diplomatic signals from Secretary of State nominee Marco Rubio, who favors confrontation over engagement.

question: Israeli parliament dissolved by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the sitting Israeli Knesset (Israel's parliament), as of this market's inception, is dissolved between September 20 and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Israeli parliament dissolved by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the sitting Israeli Knesset (Israel's parliament), as of this market's inception, is dissolved between September 3 and October 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Coalition fractures within Prime Minister Netanyahu's government, especially over a failed ultra-Orthodox military draft exemption bill, are the main drivers of trader sentiment on Knesset dissolution odds. The bill's defeat in late June 2024 prompted threats from key allies like National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir to withdraw support, risking early elections amid the ongoing Gaza conflict and hostage negotiations. Polls show opposition parties leading significantly, boosting yes bets, while Netanyahu maneuvers to hold the coalition. A critical upcoming deadline looms: failure to pass the 2025 state budget by March 31 triggers automatic dissolution. War developments and U.S. election outcomes could further sway probabilities, underscoring high uncertainty in Israel's polarized politics.

question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day?
description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no OpenAI IPO by December 31, 2026, at 64% implied probability, primarily due to the company's complex capped-profit structure under nonprofit oversight, ongoing private funding rounds—like recent talks for a $6.5 billion raise valuing it at $150 billion post-money—and Sam Altman's repeated statements prioritizing long-term AI safety over public markets. Among IPO scenarios, 1T–1.25T odds (9.3%) lead, buoyed by explosive revenue growth to $3.7 billion annualized run-rate from GPT models and enterprise deals, though regulatory scrutiny on AI monopolies and Microsoft’s deep stake temper enthusiasm. No firm IPO timeline has emerged post-October funding news, reinforcing private status quo.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Fannie Mae IPO by June 30, 2026 (89.5% implied probability), driven by entrenched regulatory complexities despite President-elect Trump's post-election pledge to end the GSE's conservatorship. With $83 billion net worth backing a $4.3 trillion mortgage portfolio, a 200–250B market cap outcome at 7% reflects trader skepticism on near-term execution amid potential leadership shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Freddie Mac IPO by June 30, 2026, at 95.3% implied probability, driven by entrenched conservatorship since the 2008 financial crisis and formidable barriers to privatization. Recapitalization requires Treasury approval, congressional legislation, and FHFA overhaul—processes historically spanning years, as seen in stalled 2019 efforts. Real capital backing this view reflects macroeconomic caution amid elevated U.S. debt levels and mortgage market stability risks from rushed release. Challenges include a pro-privatization administration post-2024 election accelerating reforms, though the 18-month timeline to resolution remains improbably compressed without bipartisan support. Low odds on cap ranges underscore minimal near-term exit pricing expectations.

This market will resolve based on Freddie Mac's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Freddie Mac’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Freddie Mac IPO by June 30, 2026, at 95.3% implied probability, driven by entrenched conservatorship since the 2008 financial crisis and formidable barriers to privatization. Recapitalization requires Treasury approval, congressional legislation, and FHFA overhaul—processes historically spanning years, as seen in stalled 2019 efforts. Real capital backing this view reflects macroeconomic caution amid elevated U.S. debt levels and mortgage market stability risks from rushed release. Challenges include a pro-privatization administration post-2024 election accelerating reforms, though the 18-month timeline to resolution remains improbably compressed without bipartisan support. Low odds on cap ranges underscore minimal near-term exit pricing expectations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Databricks IPO by June 30, 2026 (93.1% implied probability), driven by the absence of an S-1 filing and CEO Ali Ghodsi's repeated emphasis on prioritizing growth over a rushed public debut amid a cautious tech IPO market. Supporting this positioning are Databricks' robust private funding—$500 million raised at a $43 billion valuation in late 2023—accelerating revenue to over $2.4 billion annualized run rate, and strategic acquisitions like MosaicML bolstering its AI data platform without needing public capital. Realistic challenges include an unexpected S-1 submission triggered by M&A ambitions or a broader equity market rally mirroring 2021, potentially compressing timelines despite historical product delays in enterprise tech.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no Anthropic IPO by June 30, 2026 (94.3% implied probability), anchored by the absence of any S-1 filing, roadshow, or official statements signaling public market plans from CEO Dario Amodei or the board. Recent developments reinforce this: Anthropic's $18.4 billion private valuation from mid-2024 funding, bolstered by Amazon's $4 billion commitment and Google's investments, affords multi-year runway amid Claude model's enterprise growth, while AI safety priorities and intensifying regulatory scrutiny from the FTC deter near-term listings. Upside caps like 600B+ garner just 2.1% due to lofty benchmarks versus peers like OpenAI's rumored $150B private mark. Challenges include explosive revenue beats or a frothy IPO window post-2025 rate cuts, but historical AI startup timelines (e.g., delayed Databricks) suggest inertia prevails.

question: Will Kylian Mbappé win the 2026 Ballon d'Or?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Ballon d’Or.

If no 2026 Ballon d’Or winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from France Football (https://www.francefootball.fr/).

events.title: Ballon d'Or Winner 2026
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Ballon d’Or.

If no 2026 Ballon d’Or winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from France Football (https://www.francefootball.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for the 2026 Ballon d'Or reflects high uncertainty this early, with Lamine Yamal's 30% implied probability barely ahead of Harry Kane (24.9%) and Kylian Mbappé (24.0%), driven by their breakout trajectories amid a wide-open field. Yamal's Euro 2024 stardom for champion Spain and strong Barcelona start highlight his rising narrative, while Kane's prolific Bayern scoring consistency and Mbappé's blockbuster Real Madrid transfer sustain their bids. The race stays tight as the 2025-26 season unfolds, with club titles, Champions League runs, and the 2026 World Cup poised to shift dynamics through goal tallies, assists, and team successes—factors where upsets remain common.

question: US recession by end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if either of the following conditions is met:

1.  The seasonally adjusted annualized percent change in quarterly U.S. real GDP from the previous quarter is less than 0.0 for two consecutive quarters between Q2 2025 and Q4 2026 (inclusive), as reported by the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). 

2.  The National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) publicly announces that a recession has occurred in the United States, at any point during 2025 or 2026, with the announcement made by the time the BEA releases the advance estimate for Q4 2026.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

Note that advance estimates will be considered. For example, if upon release, the advance estimate for Q3 2025 was negative, and the Q2 2025's most recent, up-to-date estimate was also negative, this market would resolve to "Yes". If on December 31, 2026 the latest estimate for quarterly GDP in Q3 2025 was negative, this market will stay open until the Advance estimate of Q4 2026 is published, at which point it will resolve to "Yes" if Q4 2026 was negative or if the NBER declares a recession by then.

The resolution source will be the official announcements from the NBER and the BEA’s estimate of seasonally adjusted annualized percent change in quarterly US real GDP from previous quarters as released by the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

2.  The National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) publicly announces that a recession has occurred in the United States, at any point during 2025 or 2026, with the announcement made by the time the BEA releases the advance estimate for Q4 2026.

The resolution source will be the official announcements from the NBER and the BEA’s estimate of seasonally adjusted annualized percent change in quarterly US real GDP from previous quarters as released by the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price a 64.5% probability of no US recession by end-2026, driven primarily by the economy's resilient soft landing trajectory amid Federal Reserve rate cuts. September's BLS report showed unemployment steady at 4.1% with 254,000 nonfarm payroll gains, while Q3 GDP expanded 2.8% annualized per BEA, outpacing expectations and signaling robust consumer spending and corporate investment. Cooling core PCE inflation at 2.7% year-over-year enables the Fed's 50bps cut in September, with markets implying 80% odds of another 25bps reduction in November. Yield curve normalization and low bank stress further bolster consensus, though risks linger from geopolitical tensions and potential fiscal tightening post-election; watch December FOMC and Q4 GDP for shifts.

question: NATO/EU troops fighting in Ukraine in June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if active military personnel officially affiliated with any NATO or EU country enter Ukraine for combat-related military purposes directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, they must be 1) officially acknowledged as active military by the NATO or EU entity or member state they are affiliated with; 2) be publicly acknowledged by NATO or an EU-affiliated entity to have entered Ukraine for a combat-related military purpose directly pertaining to the ongoing conflict with Russia.

For military personnel to qualify toward a "Yes" resolution they need be active duty and acknowledged as described above. Participation in a combat role is necessary for this market to resolve to "Yes" (e.g. military personnel providing training or intelligence support would not qualify toward a "Yes" resolution, however drone pilots or infantry directly attacking Russian troops, or soldiers targeting and downing missiles from Ukrainian soil would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution).

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from NATO, the EU, or member states of either entity, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on low odds for NATO or EU combat troops deploying to fight in Ukraine stems from alliance leaders' repeated commitments to avoid direct confrontation with Russia, emphasizing aid, training, and weapons over boots on the ground. Recent U.S. President-elect Trump's victory has amplified de-escalation signals, with his team pushing ceasefire talks and reduced aid, while NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte reaffirmed no troop deployments in December statements. Russian advances in Donetsk add pressure for negotiations, not escalation. Traders eye January's U.S. inauguration and potential Putin-Trump dialogues as pivotal, alongside stalled EU training missions limited to Ukraine's borders, maintaining slim probabilities amid high nuclear risks.

question: Will Putin meet with Zelenskyy by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Vladimir Putin meets with Volodymyr Zelenskyy between September 23 ET, and June 30, 2026 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Zelenskyy and Putin are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Putin meet with Zelenskyy by June 30, 2026?
events.description:

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Zelenskyy and Putin are present and interact with each other in person.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russia-Ukraine war stalemate and irreconcilable demands drive the 96.2% trader consensus against a Putin-Zelenskyy meeting by June 30, 2026, as both leaders maintain hardline positions with no direct talks since the 2022 invasion. Putin has repeatedly conditioned any summit on Ukraine ceding annexed territories, halting NATO aspirations, and recognizing Russia's security concerns, demands Zelenskyy rejects outright, insisting on full territorial restoration and withdrawal of Russian forces. Recent diplomatic efforts, including indirect U.S.-mediated overtures and European shuttle diplomacy, have yielded no breakthroughs, reinforcing trader confidence in prolonged deadlock. Realistic shifts could arise from a major ceasefire, U.S. policy changes post-2024 election, or unexpected escalation forcing talks, though historical patterns suggest low odds of direct engagement within the timeframe.

question: Will Russia invade a NATO country by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Russia commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of any NATO country by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by NATO member states or Russia as of May 28, 2025, will be considered the sovereign territory of those countries.

Hybrid or “grey zone” operations conducted by unmarked personnel will qualify if they are confirmed to be Russian operatives and are intended to establish de facto control over territory, as occurred in Crimea (2014) or Donbas (2014–2022).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will Russia invade a NATO country by...?

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by NATO member states or Russia as of May 28, 2025, will be considered the sovereign territory of those countries.

Hybrid or “grey zone” operations conducted by unmarked personnel will qualify if they are confirmed to be Russian operatives and are intended to establish de facto control over territory, as occurred in Crimea (2014) or Donbas (2014–2022).

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: NATO's reinforced deterrence on its eastern flank, including multinational battlegroups in the Baltics and Poland, remains the primary factor suppressing trader consensus on a Russian invasion of a NATO member before the market's resolution. Russia's ongoing offensive in Ukraine has stretched its conventional forces, with no verified intelligence indicating preparations for Article 5-triggering aggression against alliance territory. Recent developments include Finland and Sweden's full NATO integration, boosting collective defense, and heightened hybrid threats like sabotage in Europe, but these fall short of invasion thresholds. Upcoming U.S. elections in November 2024 could influence alliance cohesion, while NATO's July summit commitments for sustained Ukraine aid signal unity. Uncertainty persists amid Putin's rhetoric, yet evidence points to contained escalation risks.

question: Will Ukraine recapture Crimean territory by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Ukraine captures any territory in Crimea by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will will be considered captured if any part of Crimea shaded blue on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

The border around Crimea shaded in black on the ISW map will not qualify. 

Once Ukraine captures territory in Crimea, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Ukraine comes into control of territory in Crimea as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of if it is shaded blue in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement which gives Ukraine de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map which will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russia's entrenched fortifications in Crimea, including dense air defenses, minefields, and the Sevastopol naval base housing its Black Sea Fleet, form the primary barrier to Ukrainian recapture, driving trader consensus to a 96.9% implied probability of "No" by June 30, 2026. Ukraine's 2023 counteroffensive stalled with minimal gains, while recent Kursk region incursions represent tactical diversions rather than strategic progress toward the peninsula. Ongoing Russian advances in Donbas and limited Western aid escalation further solidify high confidence in the status quo. Realistic shifts could stem from massive surges in long-range weapons or F-16 deployments enabling air superiority, or unforeseen Russian internal instability, though these remain low-probability amid current stalemate dynamics.

question: Ukraine recognizes Russian sovereignty over its territory by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a deal in which Ukraine agrees to formally recognize Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory is reached between the Russian Federation and Ukraine by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The deal must be a publicly announced mutual agreement. 

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory for any amount of time will count.

An agreement by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995, “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia, which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement, is an example of a qualifying agreement.

An official unilateral pledge by Ukraine formally recognizing Russian sovereignty over any UN-recognized Ukrainian territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution even if not part of an agreement with Russia.

The April 19, 2013, Brussels Agreement between Serbia and Kosovo, in which Serbia recognized Kosovo’s de facto administration but did not formally grant de jure recognition, would not be considered a qualifying agreement under this market, as this market requires formal recognition of sovereignty rather than acknowledgment of administrative control.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by Ukraine and/or the Russian Federation, however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's firm insistence on restoring its 1991 borders, including Crimea and Donbas, remains the dominant factor suppressing trader consensus on recognition of Russian sovereignty claims, with markets pricing near-zero implied probabilities for near-term outcomes. President Zelenskyy has repeatedly rejected territorial concessions in recent addresses, bolstered by Western military aid exceeding $100 billion since 2022 and NATO's July 2024 summit pledges for sustained support. Russia's demands for formal acknowledgment of annexed regions persist unmet amid stalled peace talks, while Ukraine's August Kursk incursion underscores its resolve. Upcoming U.S. presidential election in November could alter aid flows, potentially pressuring negotiations, though Kyiv prioritizes full withdrawal over concessions.

question: Ukraine election called by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Ukrainian presidential election is scheduled by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Ukrainian election is announced between February 14, 2025, and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Whether the election is supposed to take place during this timeframe or later will have no effect on the resolution of this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Ukraine election called by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the next Ukrainian presidential election is scheduled in 2025. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is about whether a date for the next Ukrainian election is announced between February 14 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Whether the election is supposed to take place in 2025 or later will have no effect on the resolution to this market.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the Government of Ukraine however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ukraine's presidential election remains indefinitely postponed under martial law, extended by parliament through February 2025 amid the ongoing Russian invasion, prohibiting national votes per constitutional law. President Zelenskyy's term, originally ending May 2024, continues without contest, as he insists polls must await frontline stability and full territorial control. Recent polling shows his support at 60-65%, down from wartime highs, with military chief Zaluzhnyi gaining traction among voters favoring elections sooner. Trader odds reflect skepticism on near-term developments, pricing low probability of a call before mid-2025, hinging on ceasefire talks potentially accelerated by U.S. policy shifts post-Trump inauguration. Key watch: January parliamentary vote on further martial law extension.

question: Ukraine election held by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if national elections for the parliament and/or presidency of Ukraine are held between February 12, 2025, and June 30, 2026, PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is based on whether national elections are actually held in Ukraine within the specified dates. Merely scheduling an election will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

If elections are officially scheduled for a date outside this market's timeframe, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Ukraine election held by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if national elections for the parliament and/or presidency of Ukraine are held between February 12 and December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is based on whether national elections are actually held in Ukraine within the specified dates. Merely scheduling an election will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes".

If elections are officially scheduled for a date outside of 2025, this market will immediately resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Ukraine, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing Russian invasion and repeated parliamentary extensions of martial law remain the dominant barriers to Ukraine's presidential election, constitutionally prohibited under wartime conditions since February 2022. President Zelenskyy's term formally ended in May 2024, but parliament approved the latest 90-day extension on August 15, pushing it to mid-November amid active combat. Recent polls indicate strong public support (over 70%) for postponement until security improves, while opposition figures and some Western allies debate legitimacy but accept the delay. Traders price low near-term probabilities, reflecting consensus that elections hinge on martial law lift or ceasefire—next extension vote expected early November, with no ceasefire imminent.

question: Will Tesla release Optimus by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Tesla releases a humanoid, bipedal robot (such as Optimus) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying product is a humanoid, bipedal robot intended for consumer use and newly introduced. Non-humanoid robotics, accessories, internal factory deployments, employee-only programs, and partner/enterprise pilots do not qualify.

To be considered “released,” the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the timeframe via an official Tesla consumer channel (e.g., tesla.com) with a live checkout or paid preorder/deposit. Announcements, unveilings, demos, or waitlists with “register interest” pages without payment do not suffice. Availability in any region counts if it is open to the general public.

The primary resolution source will be official statements and materials from Tesla.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla's Q3 earnings call on October 23 confirmed CEO Elon Musk's timeline for Optimus humanoid robot: limited internal production in 2025 for factory use, with external sales not until 2026, driving trader consensus to price a mere 12% implied probability of any release by year-end 2024. Recent We, Robot event demos showcased walking and folding shirts but relied on teleoperation, underscoring pre-commercial status amid skepticism over autonomy claims. Competitive pressure from Figure AI and Boston Dynamics accelerates Tesla's ambitions, yet no shipped units or firm milestones exist before Q1 2025 updates, leaving room for surprise announcements at future events like the next shareholder meeting. Traders should watch for production prototypes in Tesla factories as a key resolution trigger.

question: Will Apple release a MacBook with cellular connectivity by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple officially releases a "MacBook" product with cellular connectivity by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying product must be named "MacBook". A product that would accomplish a similar function will not qualify, the name must actually be "MacBook".

In order to be considered released, the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the specified timeframe. An announcement or unveiling alone is not sufficient.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Apple. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Apple release a MacBook with cellular connectivity by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Apple officially releases a "MacBook" product with cellular connectivity by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A qualifying product must be named "MacBook". A product that would accomplish a similar function will not qualify, the name must actually be "MacBook".

In order to be considered released, the product must be available for purchase by the general public within the specified timeframe. An announcement or unveiling alone is not sufficient.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Apple. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty at 95.4% odds against Apple releasing a MacBook with cellular connectivity by June 30, driven primarily by the absence of any announcement or prototype reveals at WWDC 2024 last week, where focus centered on software like Apple Intelligence rather than new hardware. Credible supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo recently projected the first 5G-equipped MacBook for late 2025 or 2026, citing antenna integration challenges in slim chassis and Apple's deliberate hardware cadence. While iPads and iPhones have long offered cellular options, Macs have not, with no verified leaks or FCC filings indicating imminent shipment. Realistic wildcards include an expedited surprise launch amid modem supply surges or regulatory fast-tracks, though these appear improbable given current timelines.

question: Will no Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the exact amount of cuts of 25 basis points in 2026 by the Fed (including any cuts made during the December meeting).

Emergency rate cuts outside of scheduled FOMC meetings will also count toward the total number of cuts in 2026. This market will remain open until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, to account for any such emergency actions.

For example, if the Fed cuts rates by 50 bps after a meeting, it would be considered 2 cuts (of 25 bps each).

This market will resolve early to "No" if the specified number of cuts becomes impossible — i.e., if more cuts have already occurred than the strike in question.

Note that cuts between 1–24 bps (inclusive) will also be considered 1 rate cut.

The resolution source for this market will be FOMC statements after meetings scheduled in 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm. The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

events.title: How many Fed rate cuts in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the exact amount of cuts of 25 basis points in 2026 by the Fed (including any cuts made during the December meeting).

The resolution source for this market will be FOMC statements after meetings scheduled in 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm. The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors minimal Fed rate cuts in 2026, with 37% implied probability for zero cuts (0 bps) and 26.5% for one (25 bps), aggregating to over 63% for at most one cut, driven by post-election repricing of inflationary pressures from anticipated Trump-era tariffs, tax cuts, and fiscal stimulus. This hawkish tilt contrasts the FOMC's December 2024 dot plot median of roughly two 25 bps cuts to 2.9% by year-end, as robust November jobs data (256K payrolls) and sticky core PCE inflation near 2.7% underscore economic resilience delaying deeper easing. Traders eye Q1 2025 CPI releases and January FOMC for pivotal shifts, with market-implied odds reflecting capital-backed caution on aggressive policy normalization.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors minimal Fed rate cuts in 2026, with 37% implied probability for zero cuts (0 bps) and 26.5% for one (25 bps), aggregating to over 63% for at most one cut, driven by post-election repricing of inflationary pressures from anticipated Trump-era tariffs, tax cuts, and fiscal stimulus. This hawkish tilt contrasts the FOMC's December 2024 dot plot median of roughly two 25 bps cuts to 2.9% by year-end, as robust November jobs data (256K payrolls) and sticky core PCE inflation near 2.7% underscore economic resilience delaying deeper easing. Traders eye Q1 2025 CPI releases and January FOMC for pivotal shifts, with market-implied odds reflecting capital-backed caution on aggressive policy normalization.

question: Will 9 Fed rate cuts happen in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the exact amount of cuts of 25 basis points in 2026 by the Fed (including any cuts made during the December meeting).

Emergency rate cuts outside of scheduled FOMC meetings will also count toward the total number of cuts in 2026. This market will remain open until December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, to account for any such emergency actions.

For example, if the Fed cuts rates by 50 bps after a meeting, it would be considered 2 cuts (of 25 bps each).

This market will resolve early to "No" if the specified number of cuts becomes impossible — i.e., if more cuts have already occurred than the strike in question.

Note that cuts between 1–24 bps (inclusive) will also be considered 1 rate cut.

The resolution source for this market will be FOMC statements after meetings scheduled in 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm. The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.
